channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Even the ‘Avatar’ Fighting Game Is Disrespecting ‘Legend of Korra’
item.description: The roster for 'Avatar Legends' doesn't play favorites, but at launch, it doesn't really care for 'Legend of Korra.'

item.title: ‘Project Hail Mary’ becomes Amazon MGM’s biggest box office hit
item.description: Amazon’s bet on “Project Hail Mary” has paid off handsomely.

item.title: Red Rooms makes online poker as thrilling as its serial killer
item.description: It's rare for a movie to get technology right. And it's even rarer for that movie to be a thriller or horror, where realism takes a backseat to scares and tension. But Red Rooms mostly gets it. Nothing takes me out of a film quicker than a tech MacGuffin that might as well be literal […]

item.title: Bluesky Has a New App, and It’s All About AI
item.description: Jay Graber is not CEO anymore, but that doesn't mean she hasn't been busy.

item.title: 'A high-speed digital cheat sheet': Google unveils TurboQuant AI-compression algorithm, which it claims can hugely reduce LLM memory usage
item.description: Google introduces TurboQuant, a compression method that reduces memory usage and increases speed, though results depend on benchmarks and real-world implementation variability.

item.title: Bethesda is shutting down The Elder Scrolls: Blades on June 30
item.description: It's a sad day for the dozens of players still grinding The Elder Scrolls: Blades. Bethesda announced that it's permanently shutting down the servers for its free-to-play mobile spinoff on June 30. First spotted on Reddit, The Elder Scrolls: Blades has already been delisted from the App Store and Google Play store, and is currently unavailable on the Nintendo Store. In the meantime, players will receive a free bundle of Gems and Sigils, while all items in the in-game store are available for just one Gem or Sigil each. With a server shutdown imminent, The Elder Scrolls: Blades' will at least cr...

item.title: Dammit, Jim, He’s a Doctor *And* an Action Figure!
item.description: The 60th anniversary celebration of 'Star Trek' continues with a new figure for everyone's favorite surly medical officer.
